subject: former child actors
one_liner: Short-form video for people whose IMDb page stops in 2004.
brand_voice_notes: Sincere founder pitch aimed at people whose fame has a copyright date. Treats faded recognition as an asset class and a mall opening as a booking. Never jokes, never winks; the platform believes in these careers more than anyone left in the industry does. Copy reads like a movie-palace program printed by a growth team.

hero.sub: Residual is the short-form video platform where being recognizable in 1999 is a distribution strategy. Post the scene. Post it again. The algorithm remembers you.

features.title: Peak Detection
features.blurb: We identify the eleven months your career mattered and route your videos exclusively to people who owned a television at the time. Reach that remembers.

features.title: The Typecast Engine
features.blurb: Every upload is auto-tagged with the one role you will never escape. Casting directors filter by it. Eventually, so will you.

features.title: Residual-Linked Scheduling
features.blurb: Connect your SAG-AFTRA account. When a residual check under two dollars clears, we schedule a post within the hour. Momentum is momentum.

pricing_tiers.name: General Admission
perks: Post up to 3 re-enactments per week
One (1) pinned clip from your peak

pricing_tiers.name: Mezzanine
perks: Priority placement in Where Are They Now

pricing_tiers.name: Above the Line
perks: A human employee watches every video
Quarterly call with someone who used to work in casting

testimonials.quote: I booked a mall opening within six days of joining. A mall opening.
testimonials.name: Trey Kowalczyk
testimonials.title: Third lead, two-season Nickelodeon ensemble (2001-2003)

testimonials.quote: Someone recognized me at a Costco and the app had logged it before I reached my car.
testimonials.name: Mackenzie Vole
testimonials.title: Former 'and introducing' credit, 1997

testimonials.quote: My agent has not called since 2011. Residual notifies me twice a day.
testimonials.name: Dax Pemberton
testimonials.title: Voice of an animated toaster, 1994-1996

images.rationale: Demo is photo-shaped (a For You feed of re-enactment video stills), so the whole budget went to three 9:16 feed portraits. Hero skipped because the design direction is typographic deco marquee. Testimonial headshots skipped to keep the press-clipping treatment consistent across all three quotes and because only one slot remained.

items.prompt: Vertical cinematic editorial photograph, one focal subject: a man in his mid-thirties in a modest suburban kitchen, gripping a cereal spoon and grinning with the exact rehearsed enthusiasm of a late-1990s cereal commercial, a hint of weariness in the eyes, warm tungsten key light, deep velvet maroon shadow tones near hex 211016, faded marquee-gold highlights near hex E3B341, nostalgic faded-glamour mood, shallow depth of field. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Vertical cinematic editorial photograph, one focal subject: a woman around thirty seated inside a parked car at night, performing a single dramatic tear with the practiced precision of a former teen-drama actress re-creating her most famous scene, composed face lit by warm tungsten glow, deep velvet maroon shadow tones near hex 211016, faded marquee-gold rim light near hex E3B341, nostalgic faded-glamour mood, shallow depth of field. Background outside the car window is pure dark bokeh only: no buildings, no storefronts, no signs, no signage of any kind. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Vertical cinematic editorial photograph, one focal subject: a man in his early forties in a beige suburban living room, frozen mid-move in a goofy signature dance from a bygone children's television show, utterly sincere committed expression, warm tungsten key light, deep velvet maroon shadow tones near hex 211016, faded marquee-gold highlights near hex E3B341, nostalgic faded-glamour mood, shallow depth of field. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

demo_screen_concept: A vertical For You feed of video cards in dark player chrome. Each card: a portrait still of a recognizable-adjacent adult, username, a 'Peaked in [year]' badge, a caption like 'doing the cereal line again since you asked', and a right-rail action column where the heart is replaced by 'I remember you' (tapping increments the count), plus 'Duet with your younger self' and share. A comment drawer slides up with comments like 'wait THAT kid??'. Bottom tab bar: For You / Where Are They Now / Residuals, with the Residuals tab showing a $1.47 pending balance.

design_direction.style: Art-deco faded-marquee movie palace, velvet after-hours mode. The page is staged as the program of a grand old picture house that a growth team now operates as a short-form video platform. A deep velvet maroon-black ground carries a faint vignette and a slow spotlight gradient drifting behind the hero, like a house light nobody turned off. Structure is deco: thin double keylines, stepped corner ornaments, sunburst dividers drawn in CSS, and a marquee frame around the hero headline edged with small bulb dots, exactly one of which is permanently out. One warm marquee gold carries the wordmark, headlines, structural keylines, and every fame-positive figure; one rationed spotlight rose is reserved strictly for the single primary CTA, the live 'I remember you' affordance, and the 'now casting' chip, and appears almost nowhere else. Limelight sets the wordmark and headlines in single-weight deco marquee caps, letterspaced like a theater front; Jost carries plain geometric body copy with the patience of a program note. Every figure that pretends to be career telemetry (remember-counts, peak years, residual balances, tier prices, recognition events) is set in Courier Prime with tabular feel inside small keyline chips, so it reads like a call sheet, not a marketing number. Motion is house-light slow: marquee bulbs shimmer on a 6s cycle, the spotlight drifts, feed cards rise 8px and fade in on scroll, the remember-counter ticks upward, the CTA presses down 1px, all gated behind prefers-reduced-motion. No glass, no neon bloom, no scanlines, no paper grain, no bento tiles, no hard offset shadows, no light mode; the theater is closed, the sign is still on. Reads like a heavily funded consumer app that flatters its entire user base with golden-age gravitas because the data says flattery retains.
design_direction.archetype: Consumer social-video landing staged as a movie-palace program: marquee hero with bulb-edged frame and a phone-shaped feed mockup, features as numbered playbill acts, testimonials as press-clipping reviews, pricing as ticket tiers, box-office CTA band; demo is a dark player-chrome For You feed.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Marquee bulbs shimmer on a 6s cycle with one bulb permanently dark; a soft spotlight gradient drifts behind the hero; cards rise 8px and fade on scroll; the 'I remember you' counter ticks; CTA presses down 1px on click; everything respects prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Split hero. Left: bulb-edged marquee frame holding the Limelight headline, program-note sub in Jost, single rose box-office CTA. Right: a phone-shaped demo mockup showing one For You card with a 'Peaked in 1999' badge and the 'I remember you' rail, linking to demo.html.
